constraints: Budget around $30,000; avoid features not used.
Prioritize long-term dependability and resale value; less emphasis on flashy tech.
Consider both brand-new and gently pre-owned options if it makes long-term sense.

When evaluating used options among suggested models, monitor model-specific red flags (RAV4: service history, hybrid battery/inverter, transmission issues; CR-V: maintenance records, oil dilution on 1.5L turbo, transmission/engine warning lights; Forester: oil leaks, coolant consumption, rust or flood damage).
Prefer simpler dashboard interfaces (analog gauges) and fewer electronics; avoid excessive digital displays where possible.
Ensure vehicle width including mirrors fits in narrow parking spots and minimizes risk of scraping sides.
Dealership customer experience, including aspects such as coffee quality, influences evaluation and decisions.
Cup holder size and accessibility for travel mugs; easy to clean.
open: Test drive checklist: verify cup holder fit for travel mugs; assess reach and ease of cleaning.
